reviews.review_description:
This could be a good game if you could play at your own pace.  It's a game,  you play to have fun not be forced to finish within a time frame. The graphics are a bit sketchy.  Watson is way too close and when I tried physically to move away he was still too close. And when he moves it gets fuzzy and is like it is shimmering around the edges of his body; I was close to getting a headache from this. 
Also, I know it says the multi player is experimental,  but could you put in an invite for when you create your room you can invite your friends instead of waiting for others to join. I prefer to play with friends instead of strangers,  and don't put a set number of people (you have 4) as me and my sister want to play together by ourselves. You can set a max number of 4 but allow 2 or 3 also.  We ended up playing singly on our own but because we are able to talk we managed to play at the same time. It would be much better if we were in the room at the same time. 
This seems like it should be a beta game and not one I had to pay for as it seems your still trying to work out the bugs. 
I'm sure you can do better,  I'd like to give it 5 stars , i just can't until it gets better.

This could be an amazing game if only there was a setting for relax mode.  You get so little time to solve a considerably important crime. They give you no time to look at the evidence and they give you five minutes to pick the murderer. Everything goes way too fast. There’s no time to think or figure anything out if it had a relaxed mood this game would be by far the best crime scene investigation game out there

reviews.review_title: A LITTLE GEM! THEATRICAL MYSTERY IN VR
reviews.review_description:
This experience elegantly blends interactive theater and VR immersion. Examining crime scenes and interrogating suspects is captivating, unique, and refreshing.
I was blown away by the "theater-in-your-living-room" aspect of Sherlock Holmes VR.
From the first few minutes, you're immersed in the atmosphere of a high-end thriller. Three ministers found dead, Sherlock missing, the clock is ticking… You question, take notes, and close your eyes to piece together the clues in your "Mental Fortress." The immersion truly works.
The FMV format with professional actors brings a rare, vibrant touch to VR, and the option to play solo or co-op with up to four players creates a real sense of detective camaraderie. Following a lead is smooth, clear, and incredibly satisfying.
A little gem of a narrative that combines theatrical elegance and VR charm, perfect for an immersive evening with friends. Note: Audio is in English only.

The first time I played I didn't pay as close attention as I should have to the written clues so I guesses and got it wrong. The second time I played I got it right. Plus it was cool to see the different responses from the interviews the second time around.

The good: The game is fun and really engaging.  The graphics are good.  And interaction is good. The mystery was a good challenge and. I really enjoyed it.

The bad: Its a bit short and the ending seems rushed, because of all of the timed based limitations. Also the scenes with Watson are cool but the users perspective is extreamly short, like being a 4 year old short, and the video is a bit out of focus or distorted.

Overall, its totally worth the current $12 price tag.  As it is developed more, I would like to see multiple mysteries to explore and less time based limitations.  The i could see it being worth around $25 or more, depending on the the number of mysteries available.

Really enjoying the game so far. To make the game a bit more immersive and enjoyable, I have a few suggestions for the developers. 
1. The video segments in the game could do with a rewind feature as well as subtitles so that you could go back if you missed a sentence or two. 
2. There should be an option where you could solve the case without a time limit (although the time limit makes sense and gives a sense of urgency). 
3. It would really make the game more immersive if you could freely move within a crime scene. 
4. Lastly, also not a very valid point; the actors (who did a stellar job) could have done with a more fitting attire to the time period.

I'm hoping that in the near future, new cases would be added as DLC content.

I thoroughly enjoyed playing this game and I think it has the potential to be a great game. 
However, the fact that everything you did was timed made it a very short game for the money. I would have loved to have spent more time in  Yard on the computer, also more time in the victim’s homes to wander about at my leisure.
The main bug I encountered was the  fact that I couldn’t put unused evidence back on the bench.

Interesting story. However, it being in early access and all, I hope this helps!
The rooms are a little weird to be in, it would feel better for the player if there was a map in which to stand and interact with the clues in the rooms. I've never made a game and don't know how it works, though.
The only bug I really encountered while playing normally was that I couldn't place 'evidence' that I deemed 'unhelpful' back onto the counter in front of the board.
I'm not sure how, but I somehow knew who it was the moment you showed all the suspects (or at least had it down to two people). The only thing I can say is perhaps my gut picked up on a hint that was either placed intentionally or unintentionally in the scripts.
While going through the 'evidence', I had not actually read or gone through over half of it when I'd made the decision as to who it is due to the time limit and my dyslexia causing it to be hard for me to read it, so if there was perhaps a work-around for people who have a harder time reading quickly, or move at a slower pace? On the same note, however, the quick pace was also more challenging, and I learned I work better under a time limit than I anticipated; I just know some people don't do well with time limits and can't enjoy the game properly.

Summary:
-Bug: Could not place evidence on shelf in front of board.
- Notes: High anticipation of maps and better world interaction, high anticipation of added or changed story/stories.
- Consumer Requests: More characters, option to play 'easy', 'normal', or 'hard' for different ages or intelligence types, easy-to-read evidence option (some people can't read cursive or have dyslexia), and an option to play a non-timed game? Also, please please add an option for smooth-turning?

Overall good story, good concept, can't wait to see what happens once it's out of early access! I apologize if any of this was unhelpful or unwanted, but I do hope this helps even if a little bit! Keep up the good work!

developer_response.body: thank you so much for the effort to give us such detailed feedback! It really helps a lot to understand what can be improved in the experience and it is very motivating for us to know that there are people like you supporting our work!

reviews.review_title: Great for single player; not multiplayer
reviews.review_description: Playing alone was fun.  When trying to play with 2 friends, one player never was able to be seen in the game.

I just happened to run across this well put together mystery, I thought it was very well put together really glad to have happened to run across it, I take my hat off to you well done developers very well done I truly hope you're able to come up with a new case to solve would definitely purchase it.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much Coolytwo - lovely feedback like yours really makes all the hard work on this title worth it!

reviews.review_description:
Unfortunately, I cannot agree with the predominantly positive reviews, as the app still has some shortcomings: 

- There was a time latency of up to 3 minutes between my fellow player and me! This means that she couldn't see what I was doing until minutes later. 
- Sometimes we were both invisible to each other. 
- It's a pity that the spoken texts are not translated into different languages. But the fact that they are not even displayed in text form in English makes it difficult for anyone who doesn't speak English very well to understand them. 
- The font on the letters is very small and difficult to read; an overlay text would also be helpful here. 
- If you struggle to understand the language, the time limits available are far too short. This is particularly annoying when searching the Scotland Yard "computer". 
- The board is too small to pin all the clues on it. 
- And ultimately, I would have expected more from the app than just sifting through a lot of clues and then expressing a suspicion. Especially as the only way to repeat the game is to collect all the clues again. That's too boring to play again.
- For a game that is over in just two hours, the price is still too high. 

What to do: Translations into the most common languages would definitely be helpful. This should not be difficult in a time of DeepL (with which this text was also translated) and ChatGPT. The time limits should be extended, perhaps combined with difficulty levels.

What bothered me less were the non-authentic items. Of course, in the 1880s there were no modern film projectors with colour film, no examination rooms with one-sided mirrors and TV monitors or even computers. But such things naturally destroy the intended Victorian atmosphere.

developer_response.body:
Dear MadMike,
thank you so much for taking the time to write this review and even and making the effort to suggest ways to improve the experience. For a tiny team like us this means the world! <3

Multiplayer delay: It's actually the first time we encounter this bug, so we're very grateful for you reporting it. Any pointers on how to reproduce this bug would be extremely helpful to fix it. We will work on fixing this and including it on our next update. 

Translations: Yes, this is not the first time that we get this request and we have it very high up in the list for future improvements. As you suggested we are currently looking at affordable options using LLMs. We just needed to have a starting point for releasing the game, and due to team size we had to delay the subtitles task. But worry not, it's coming very soon :)

Non-authentic items: hihi, yes, this is a funny point, since we have been told how much people love it and also comments from players asking why we went a bit off-script. This is our way to make a wink-wink to the audience of Sherlock with the intention of making you smile and be surprised. So sorry it broke the magic for you. We're still happy you played it and took the time to send us your tips on how to improve it. We're immensely grateful for that! <3

reviews.review_title: Embroiled in mystery! I have chills!
reviews.review_description: This was an incredibly fun experience that made me feel like I was in an interactive theater performance. I really enjoyed the great acting and piecing together the clues. I can't wait to try with friends!

developer_response.body: Thank you very much for playing, detective! We are very happy that you are enjoying it :)

reviews.review_title: Fun Title! Need More of These!
reviews.review_description:
I’ve had a great time and I’m really enjoying the experience so far! We need more of these types of experiences on the main store. All of the horror-themed stuff and mindless shooters are getting a bit tired. I love when devs try something new and bring new experiences to VR. 

That said, I think it does need some work on the look of the scenes with the actors. There is a weirdness to the 360 video that makes the people hard to focus on and makes me a bit nauseous. I really love the actors and that’s a great part of the experience, but please fix the 360 so it’s not so stomach churning. I found myself wishing for the game parts to start because the 360 video was giving me a headache! I don’t want to dread a part of the experience. If they can fix this I will update my review and recommend to friends as well. Great start though!

Update: What a thoughtful response from the Devs! I really appreciate your response. I can’t wait to try the game again this weekend.

developer_response.body: Dear BluSkyler, thank you so much for your kind words! It means a lot to us that our efforts are appreciated. We really hope that our latest feature update where you can chose between stereoscopic and monoscopic 360 videos helps with the difficulties that you had watching the Dr. Watson scenes :)

reviews.review_description: It's very well made, and I liked the mix of 350 videos and VR environment! This type of mixture of narrative and game is not yet very common and it was very enteresting and fun. Great work by the devs!

developer_response.body: Thank you for your kind review! We are very happy that you enjoyed this experience :)

reviews.review_description: Please can you fix the map when I press the map I can't go any further is a bug on the map so please can you fix it

developer_response.body:
Hi Devil!
VR thrilled you've tried SHTCOFTP and that you're giving us feedback! :)
We'd be happy to know: 
A- in what part of the game you've found this bug  in the map, 
B- what exactly you're trying to do with the map, and 
C- if you could send us a screenshot, if possible, to sherlockvr@vrisch.com 
One question we need to ask: you've already tried the X on the top right of the map, right? 
Thank you for playing :)
-Axel & Gabs (Sherlock team)

----- update 2023/03/07 -----

We have found and fixed a bug that caused the game to stay stuck when you select Dr. Gray on the map after Watson asks you to chose how you want to continue your investigation.
@Devil: did this fix the issue that you reported?

reviews.review_description: Very well made. Great experience for people that like escape games and/or adventure games.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much, detective! VR thrilled you enjoyed our mystery quest! 🕵️🔍

reviews.review_description:
PLEASE, I BEG YOU: I simply love this type of games and I love Sherlock Holmes but I am not a native speaker. I am not going to ask you to translate the voice but, at least, insert English subtitles!
It should be quite inexpensive and it could permit to almost everyone to enjoy the game (it is not an arcade: every word must be understood!!!)

developer_response.body: Oh, goodness us. Our manners! So sorry to keep you waiting for a reply, Boggi! Somehow we weren't notified of your important message. Thank you for your valuable feedback. Your suggestion is immensely important to us as we want to improve the game upon needs, suggestions, and feature requests from players, so we will definitely work on this in our future updates. Thank you so much for taking the time to leave us this request, and we hope you're enjoying the game as much as we enjoyed making it for you <3 All the best, detective!

reviews.review_description: Step into London‘s finest address and solve the Mystery . Very enjoyable. Super actors. Great fun

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for your feedback :) It really means a lot to us and we are very happy that you enjoyed the experience!

reviews.review_description: This is just like being in an immersive theatre performance though with puzzles thrown in - so well done! Once I started I played all the way through non stop as it was just so good. The acting was excellent as well as the graphics. More please!!

developer_response.body: Thank you so much JodieSummer for your lovely review! It fills our hearts with joy to receive feedback like yours :)

reviews.review_description: Funny, mysterious, can be played with multiple people and also challenging.
